questions.question: Describe the primary purpose of controlled access in computer networks. Explain how it differs from open access. (10 marks)
What are the main advantages of using token passing as a method of controlled access? Provide an example of how token passing works in a network. (10 marks)

A network using token passing has 10 nodes. Each node is assigned a unique token and a token can be passed to the next node only when the current node has finished transmitting. What happens when a new node joins the network? How many attempts will it take for a new node to gain access to the network? (5 marks)
